name: Multiple Sensitive Resources Deleted
notes:
This rule triggers when multiple sensitive resources are being deleted. This may indicate an intruder is compromising sensitive information.

Note: The Sensitive Resource Names reference map of sets must be populated with the relevant names.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Containers

when the event category for the event is one of the following Audit.Network Deletion Success, Authentication.Access Key Removal Success, Authentication.Role Removal Success, Authentication.Role Unassignment Success, Audit.Machine Deletion Success, System.Task Cancellation Success, Audit.Delete Activity Succeeded, System.Cron Failed
when any of Resource (custom) is the key and any of Resource Name (custom) is the value in any of Sensitive Resource Names - AlphaNumeric
when at least 5 events are seen with the same Source Address and different Resource Name (custom) in 24 hour(s)
thresholds: when at least 5 events are seen with the same Source Address and different Resource Name (custom) in 24 hour(s)
TD_list: APPLY Multiple Sensitive Resources Deleted on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Containers

AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Audit.Network Deletion Success, Authentication.Access Key Removal Success, Authentication.Role Removal Success, Authentication.Role Unassignment Success, Audit.Machine Deletion Success, System.Task Cancellation Success, Audit.Delete Activity Succeeded, System.Cron Failed
AND when any of Resource (custom) is the key and any of Resource Name (custom) is the value in any of Sensitive Resource Names - AlphaNumeric
AND when at least 5 events are seen with the same Source Address and different Resource Name (custom) in 24 hour(s)
RED_list: Event name: Multiple Sensitive Resources Deleted
Event description: Detects when multiple sensitive resources are deleted.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
